_meta.notes: A cathedral built into a salt cave on the cliff coast. Five rooms; the cliff path is the way out. Built by people who needed god to taste of something. The wyrm has touched this place — salt is its sign.

tagline: They built god a place where every breath tastes of the sea.
ambientPool: The salt walls hum at a frequency that is almost a voice.
Somewhere far in, drips fall into a basin that has been catching them for centuries.
Air moves toward the cathedral, not away from it.
The light here is reflected from the sea outside, cast across the salt in slow ripples.

rooms.summary: The entrance is a fissure in the cliff. Salt-rimed steps go down. Behind you, the sea. Ahead, the cathedral.
ambientPool: The steps are salt-eaten and pitted. Some hands smoothed them; sea has done the rest.
A bronze ring is set into the rock, but no rope hangs from it now.
A single gull screams once, far, and is silent.
rooms.exitNarrative: You leave the cathedral. The cliff path is steep. The sea is wide.

rooms.summary: A long hall carved into a single salt seam. Pews, also salt. They will dissolve in the next storm, and have been promised to.
ambientPool: A pew has been pushed askew by something that did not respect pews.
The salt of the floor has been worn into a path by feet older than yours.
From the altar at the far end, something glints.

rooms.summary: A raised salt block. On it, a basin half-full of seawater, fresh from this morning. Beside the basin, a knife. Fresh-sharpened.
ambientPool: The basin's surface trembles in a way no draft would cause.
The knife's edge is so fine it looks unfinished.
Behind the altar, the salt darkens — there has been a stain.

hazards.narrative: Whatever has used this altar recently was not human. The stain is old by hours, not centuries.

exits.narrative: Down behind the altar, where the salt parts.

rooms.summary: A smaller room. A single stone bench. A salt rendering of a figure — three eyes, no mouth — in the wall.
ambientPool: The figure's three eyes have been touched many times. The salt around them is smoother.
On the bench, a child's wooden bead. Recent.
The light in this room comes from no source you can locate.

rooms.summary: Behind the altar, the salt has parted into a chamber the architects did not know about. The walls here are wet. The hum is louder.
ambientPool: There is a coil here, vast, only a section of which is visible against the salt.
The salt in this chamber tastes different. Older. Wronger.
Whatever is here knows your name now.

hazards.narrative: Something coiled. Something patient. Direct provocation will not survive the chamber.
